text: Code reviews are an essential part of the software development process. When a developer finishes writing a piece of code, another team member examines it carefully. The main benefit is catching bugs early, before they reach users. This saves time and money, as fixing problems later is much harder. Additionally, code reviews encourage knowledge sharing. Developers learn new techniques and see different ways to solve problems. This helps spread expertise across the team, so no one person holds all the knowledge about a particular part of the system. Ultimately, regular code reviews improve overall team quality by promoting cleaner, more reliable code and stronger collaboration.
